title: collisionspike UX Design Lab — scoring rubric
appliedBy: design-critic (+ accessibility-engineer for the accessibility dimension)

anchors.1: Token / broken — present in name only.

scoring.perDimension: 0-5 integer, justified in one sentence with a screen reference.
scoring.total: Weighted sum, normalised to 100. Report both the weighted total and the raw per-dimension vector.
gates: accessibility < 2 caps the overall result at 'not shippable' regardless of weighted total.
featureCoverage < 3 disqualifies from winner selection (it cannot represent the product).

dimensions.label: Feature coverage
dimensions.definition: Renders the full Phases 1-9 screen/feature inventory (design-brief.md S1-S17) — inbox cockpit, triage, queues, case detail with all 12 EVA fields + evidence + provenance + readiness + chasers + address, submit, admin/governance, and gated features in honest disabled states.
dimensions.anchor5: Every S1-S17 surface has a home and the load-bearing screens (S1-S9, S13, S15) are fully realised.
dimensions.anchor0: Whole feature areas (e.g. triage, admin, or evidence/photo order) are missing.

dimensions.label: Task efficiency
dimensions.definition: How few steps and how little friction the top jobs-to-be-done cost (design-brief.md §7) — triage an email, review a case to ready, chase a partial, submit to EVA — including deep-links, keyboard paths, and the three-kinds-of-number discipline that keeps drainable work obvious.
dimensions.anchor5: Each core flow is a short, obvious path; the cockpit makes 'what do I do next' answerable at a glance.
dimensions.anchor0: Common tasks require hunting, redundant navigation, or conflate backlog with throughput.

dimensions.label: Intuitiveness
dimensions.definition: A first-time intake operator understands the layout, the queues-by-who-acts-next model, status, provenance, and readiness without training; things are named by what people control, and empty/error states give direction.
dimensions.anchor5: Self-evident IA and labels; the next action is always discoverable.
dimensions.anchor0: Jargon, ambiguous nav, or states that leave the user stuck.

dimensions.label: Visual appeal
dimensions.definition: Distinctive, intentional, professional craft — typography, hierarchy, spacing, restraint — that does not read as a templated default and earns trust for a claims-intake tool.
dimensions.anchor5: A confident, memorable visual identity with disciplined hierarchy.

dimensions.label: Relevance to the finished product
dimensions.definition: Fit to what collisionspike actually is — a single-operator Power Apps case-intake workspace over real Dataverse data and gated integrations — not a generic dashboard; honours the binding business rules (design-brief.md §6).
dimensions.anchor5: Reads as this product; respects the status machine, readiness gate, photo order, no-silent-merge, gated honesty.
dimensions.anchor0: A pretty mock that ignores the domain or contradicts a binding rule.

dimensions.label: Brand re-anchorability
dimensions.definition: How cleanly the direction can be re-skinned to the Collision Engineers brand at port — CE-red #db0816 as a budgeted accent, Futura display-only, 2px radii, charcoal rail as system chrome — without the design collapsing.
dimensions.anchor5: Structure survives a CE re-skin as pixels, not just spirit.
dimensions.anchor0: Identity depends on off-brand colour/type that cannot be reconciled.

dimensions.label: Accessibility (WCAG-AA)
dimensions.definition: Meets the AA release gate — visible focus, AA contrast, colour never the sole signal (status/provenance/severity also carry icon+text), >=44px touch targets, keyboard-operable grids/reorder/dialogs, reduced-motion support.
dimensions.anchor5: Passes an axe/contrast + keyboard sweep with no blocking issues.
dimensions.anchor0: Colour-only signals, unfocusable controls, or failing contrast.

dimensions.label: Fluent v9 portability
dimensions.definition: How directly the layout maps to Fluent UI v9 primitives and the existing component library under CSP connect-src 'none' (connectors/data-seam only, no raw fetch, no iframes) — reusing VrmPlate, PipelineStrip, StatusBadge, ProvenanceBadge, ReadinessChecklist, ImageOrderList, ChaserPanel, DataGrid, Dialog.
dimensions.anchor5: Ports to Fluent v9 with low effort; relies on standard components and the CSP-safe data seam.
dimensions.anchor0: Depends on bespoke widgets, raw fetch, or iframes that cannot exist in the Code App.
